title: podman exec doesn't work with custom PATH
body:
The "exec" command does not honor the PATH (if any), of the image, like the "run" command does.

Thus, when using `exec` one _always_ gets the default PATH - using `run` gets the custom PATH.

Overriding the PATH var with `-e` works.

Seems to be a bug setting "env" twice ?

comments.body:
I wonder if it's just defaulting to whatever PATH is set in the container when it's first created - if you do a `podman run -e PATH:...`, does it use that path?

If so, it's a pretty simple matter to pull it out of the container config and use it.
Sorry, `docker run -e PATH:...` - to see how Docker handles it

Here was the duplicated "env" code I meant:

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/cmd/podman/exec.go#L103
https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/cmd/podman/exec.go#L112

When compared to the run/create version of it:

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/cmd/podman/create.go#L593
https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/cmd/podman/create.go#L602

@mheon : I wanted my go image to set up all the needed `go` environment variables:

``` sh
export GOROOT=/usr/local/go
export PATH=$GOROOT/bin:$PATH
export GOPATH=~/go
export PATH=$GOPATH/bin:$PATH
```

But when I tried to reuse my running container, suddenly it couldn't find `go` anymore.

The current workaround is to add an extra `-e` parameter, when using `exec`:

`-e PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:/home/tc/go/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin`

It is not needed for the the inital `run`, but it needed for any re-use with `exec`

`docker://boot2podman-docker-tinycore.bintray.io/tinycore-go:1.10.4`
